ORCHESTRATOR ADDENDUM (corrects the "6 pre-existing failures" note above — that wording was inaccurate):
  The 6 A11/P1/G1/H1 failures the role saw were NOT pre-existing-broken (clean origin/main passes the
  opencode suite: "opencode-edition test passed, 477 assertions"). They were PURELY ENVIRONMENTAL: a
  fresh git worktree cannot contain `.opencode/plugins/kaola-workflow-hooks.js` — `.opencode/` is fully
  gitignored (.gitignore:5), the plugin has NO tracked source and is NOT generated by
  sync-opencode-edition.js (it exists only as an untracked artifact in main's working tree). A11/H1 read
  the repo's own `.opencode/` directly and P1/G1 use install-opencode.sh which COPIES from
  `$SCRIPT_DIR/.opencode/plugins/*.js` (install-opencode.sh:170, `2>/dev/null || true` — silent no-op when
  absent). n1's diff touched NONE of: `.opencode/`, install-opencode.sh, sync-opencode-edition.js, hooks/.
  Verified by re-running the worktree's NEW test against a populated `.opencode/` (seeded plugin+hooks
  from main — both #576-untouched — then `sync-opencode-edition.js --write` to regenerate the agent/command
  mirrors from THIS worktree's STRIPPED canonical):
    $ node scripts/test-opencode-edition.js → opencode-edition test passed (494 assertions).
  494 = 477 baseline + 17 new A25 PROVENANCE_BAN assertions. So A25 did not break the harness; the strip
  is clean; the 6 failures were missing-artifact noise. The worktree `.opencode/` is now populated so n4's
  opencode-suite run is clean. (`.opencode/` is gitignored → not part of the finalize merge.)
